violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. All containers were labeled at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidence by droppings throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue to work with a licensed pest control company to eliminate pests. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings with appropriate means of sanitizing. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils were re-located at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025
Establishment is lacking proper drain plugs. Establishment bought 1 proper drain plug and will buy 2 more for all bays of the 3-bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025
Multiple floor tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Raw animal foods chicken were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods chopped onions and lettuce in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Proper food storage reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was moved to the 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was observed exceeding 200 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Chlorine concentration was fixed to 100 ppm at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Bean sprouts and lettuce was observed held at 65 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, bean sprouts and lettuce was taken out of cooler 1 hour prior to inspection. Food was placed into the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Various food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, noodles and chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Noodles and chicken were placed on to sheet pans at the time of inspection to finish cooling. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by live activity. The presence of mice is not controlled as evidence by droppings throughout the establishment and dead mouse in boiler room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue to work with a licensed pest control company to eliminate pests. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings with appropriate means of sanitizing and have pest control report for inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The door screen to the outside, located in the kitchen, was observed ripped and not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients and pork. Utensils used to dispense TCS and non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
The establishment is lacking proper drain plugs. Establishment is to obtain drain plugs. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The 3-bay sink faucet had no water available at the time of inspection. Establishment is using the spray hose to fill sinks. Establishment is to have the 3-bay sink faucet serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025
Multiple floor tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and burrows outside. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and debris. The dumpster area must be maintained clean to prevent harborage for pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
The grease recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and debris. The bin should be cleaned or replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

The floors throughout establishment have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (onions) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Several foods, which were prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Foods that were under 70 degrees Fahrenheit and made 2 hours prior were put in the freezer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Several TCS foods (see disposal form) were observed held at 46-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Eggs rolls, chicken fingers and crab rangoons were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At the time of inspection, several foods were placed hot in the walk-in cooler in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods were moved to sheet pans. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen and burrows outside. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
At the time of inspection, ambient of walk-in cooler was at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of dry storage shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and debris. The dumpster area must be maintained clean to prevent harborage for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The grease recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and debris. The bin should be cleaned or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The floors in the following areas have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. 1. Under prep unit 2. Dry storage shelving 3. Mop sink 4. Behind wok station New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
Cans of food items were observed adulterated by rodent droppings on lids. PIC disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (peeler, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by dropppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
Various were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

The following have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. 1. Grease strap 2. Mop sink 3. Floors 4. Storage room Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives, ingredient bins) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Garlic was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

Various prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. 3 Bay Sink Faucet 2. Shelving 3. Prep tables 4. Prep units New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the hoods have an accumulation of grease and soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
